trust.title: Why us
trust.nationwideSub: Our machines in factories across Thailand

home.h1: Ceramic machinery built and repaired by Thai engineers, since 1986
home.sub: We design, build, install, and stand behind every machine for its lifetime — made in Thailand.
home.featuredSub: Coverage across the full ceramic line — from raw-material grinding to glazing and forming.
home.servicesTitle: Everything machine-related, handled in one place
home.service1Body: Engineered to your spec, your floor space, your throughput.
home.service2Body: The same engineers who built the machine know every part of it.
home.service3Body: Spares made in our workshop — no waiting for overseas parts.
home.ctaTitle: Got a machine problem on the line?
home.ctaSub: Talk to us at any stage — from design and build, to install and lifetime service.

about.h1: Thai engineering the ceramic industry has trusted for 35+ years
about.p1: Petkasem Ceramic Machine was founded in 1986, starting from a 400-square-metre workshop with a team of ten. The quality of the work spoke for itself — demand grew quickly.
about.p2: In 1992 we moved to a new 3,200-square-metre factory in Nakhon Pathom with more than 40 skilled engineers, to keep up with that demand at higher quality.
about.pStandards: Our machines run in ceramic factories across Thailand — in private industry, government facilities, and academic institutions. They run because they're built to be built right, and supported for the long haul. In 1995 we were also entrusted with OEM production for an overseas brand — one of the milestones we're quietly proud of.
about.pBeyond: Beyond ceramic machinery we've expanded into food processing and other industrial equipment, sticking to the same rule: we only take on work we're confident we can stand behind.
about.pPromise: We still work the way we did on day one — make good machines, price them fairly, and look after the customer afterwards. A good machine needs people who can keep it running for the long term.

products.sub: We build machinery across the full ceramic production line — from raw-material grinding and filtration through to forming. Every machine is built to the same standard and sized to your throughput and floor space. If you don't see what you need, we make to order.

product.askForSpec: Tell us your throughput and the space you have — we'll size the machine to fit.

solutions.h1: Tell us the problem on your line — we'll tell you which machine solves it
solutions.sub: Match the symptom you're seeing to the right machine. Each row links straight to the page for that machine.

custom.h1: Off-the-shelf doesn't fit? We design and build to fit.
custom.sub: Every factory has its own process and constraints, and an off-the-shelf machine rarely fits all of them. We design and build to your spec, your space, and your throughput.
custom.why1: 35+ years of real ceramic-factory work in Thailand.
custom.why2: We build and repair in the same workshop — accountability stays in one place.
custom.why3: Fully customisable: size, power, every operational detail.
custom.why4: Talk to the engineers directly — changes happen fast, on the floor.
custom.step1: We take your brief and operating requirements.
custom.step3: Build and bench-test at our factory.
custom.step4: Install and hand over on site.
custom.step5: Continuous aftercare for the life of the machine.
custom.ctaTitle: Got a design, a sketch, or just a problem to solve?

service.h1: When the machine stops, the line stops. Call us before it gets worse.
service.sub: We service and repair ceramic machinery of every kind, with the engineers who built these machines in the first place. They know every part.
service.scope1: Ball mills, filter presses, grinders, magnetic sieves, and any other ceramic-line machinery.
service.scope2: Both machines built by us and general ceramic-factory equipment.
service.why1Body: The same engineers who build the machines diagnose them.
service.why2Body: We respond quickly to cut downtime.
service.why3Body: We make and source our own parts — no waiting on imports.
service.why4Body: Preventive maintenance planning to keep machines running long.

contact.h1: Let's talk about your build, repair, or quote
contact.sub: Free consultation, no commitment. You can talk to our engineers directly.
contact.formNote: Fastest reply via LINE or phone.
contact.fieldMessage: Machine details / problem you want to solve

solutionsList.s1Pain: We need consistent fineness on raw materials and glaze
solutionsList.s1Sol: Ball Mill — steady fineness, fewer rejects
solutionsList.s2Pain: Iron specks in our slip are making blemished, scrap pieces
solutionsList.s2Sol: Magnetic Sieve — keeps slip and glaze clean
solutionsList.s3Pain: Slip is too wet — we need press-ready clay
solutionsList.s4Pain: Lumps are too hard — we need to break them down before milling
solutionsList.s4Sol: Jaw Crusher / Hammer Mill — heavy-duty size reduction
solutionsList.s5Pain: Air bubbles in the clay are cracking the fired ware
solutionsList.s5Sol: De-airing Pug Mill — removes air, homogenises the body
solutionsList.s6Pain: We need to scale up plate/bowl forming with consistent quality
solutionsList.s6Sol: Jigger / Roller-Head — fast, consistent round-ware forming
solutionsList.s7Pain: We need to form dense tile, refractory, or abrasive shapes
solutionsList.s7Sol: Hydraulic / Ram Press — high, consistent force
solutionsList.s8Pain: Glaze coverage is uneven and there's overspray everywhere
solutionsList.s8Sol: Glaze Booth — even coverage, clean workspace
solutionsList.s9Pain: Our existing machinery is breaking down or showing its age
solutionsList.s9Sol: Repair & maintenance — by the engineers who build these machines
solutionsList.s10Pain: Our problem is unusual — no off-the-shelf machine fits
solutionsList.s10Sol: Custom / OEM — we design and build to fit
